_comment: Import via Pterodactyl Admin → Nests → Eggs → Import. Use a Node.js egg nest.
name: Helmora Hub
author: helmora
description: Helmora Hub — OpenAI-compatible AI gateway (Node 20). Settings: /settings. Storage: Local SQLite or SQL Supabase.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
# Helmora Hub Pterodactyl install
set -e
cd /mnt/server

if [ ! -f package.json ]; then
  echo "Upload Helmora Hub source (or git clone) into the server directory first."
  exit 1
fi

# Prefer panel Node; yolks image provides npm/node
if command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  NPM=npm
else
  echo "npm not found in install container"
  exit 1
fi

$NPM install
$NPM run build
$NPM prune --omit=dev
mkdir -p data

if [ ! -f .env ] && [ -f .env.example ]; then
  cp .env.example .env
  echo "Created .env from .env.example — edit via File Manager"
fi

echo "============================================"
echo "Helmora Hub installed."
echo "Start command: node scripts/ptero-start.mjs"
echo "Settings UI:   http://<ip>:<port>/settings"
echo "============================================"

variables.name: App Port
variables.description: Internal listen port (Pterodactyl maps allocation → SERVER_PORT automatically in many eggs; Helmora Hub also reads SERVER_PORT).

variables.name: Encryption Key
variables.description: AES-256-GCM master key for provider API secrets. Required for SQL (Supabase) storage.

variables.name: Unified API Key
variables.description: Bearer key for /v1 clients. Leave empty to auto-generate on first boot.

variables.name: Data Directory
variables.description: Persistent data path inside the container (SQLite + runtime-config.json).

variables.name: Bind Public
variables.description: Set 1 to listen on 0.0.0.0 (recommended on panels).

variables.name: Cloudflare Tunnel Token
variables.description: Named tunnel connector token (eyJ…). Leave empty to use Settings UI or data/cloudflare-tunnel.token. Auto-starts on boot when set.

variables.name: Cloudflare Tunnel Hostname
variables.description: Public hostname for display (e.g. hub.example.com). Configure routing in Cloudflare Zero Trust.
